notes: Agent-proposed labels, hand-transcribed from the public reference Slack server. Pending human ratification. No MCP annotations are advertised. `slack_post_message` reaches an external workspace and cannot be cleanly unsent, so both a `reach-absent` and an `irreversible-unflagged` nudge are warranted.

provenance.source: github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers (Slack server) — slack_list_channels reads from the external Slack workspace API
provenance.notes: Read-only, but it interacts with an external service, so openWorldHint honestly applies. A recall check: the tool takes no destination-shaped parameter, so the v0 engine cannot see the reach from the schema and will miss it.

provenance.source: github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers (Slack server) — slack_get_channel_history reads messages from the external Slack workspace API
provenance.notes: Read-only, but it interacts with an external service, so openWorldHint honestly applies; undeclared here.

provenance.source: github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers (Slack server) — slack_post_message posts a message visible to a channel's members
provenance.notes: A posted message is externally visible and cannot be reliably unsent; both external reach and irreversibility go undeclared.
